hero.headline: The operational lifecycle
hero.body: Twelve operations connect installation to continuous evolution. Select a step to see its command, inputs, outputs and generated artifacts.

walkthrough.headline: One change through the harness
walkthrough.body: Scenario: a change touches backend/payments/**. Step through what the harness does.

steps.name: Install
steps.description: Installs the kernel into the consumer repository: templates, configuration, agents, skills, scripts and CI workflows.
steps.input: The arah-harness kernel and an existing repository.
steps.output: A governed repository with versioned contracts.

steps.name: Discover
steps.description: Observes stack, frameworks, structure, directories and domain hints to propose domains and specialists.
steps.input: The repository as it exists — code, structure, dependencies.
steps.output: A reviewable proposal of domains and specialists.

steps.name: Configure
steps.description: Humans review the proposal and set policies: autonomy levels, gates per domain, escalation rules.

steps.name: Domain Sync
steps.description: Turns approved domain candidates into explicit domains with responsibilities, paths, references and validations.

steps.name: Choreograph
steps.description: A change enters. Path-based choreography matches affected files to domains and selects agents, skills and gates.
steps.output: An activation set: agents, advisors, skills, gates.

steps.name: Execute
steps.description: Operational agents act through deterministic skills, within their declared scope and autonomy.
steps.command:
(agent session via Claude Code,
 Cursor, Copilot or custom)

steps.name: Validate
steps.description: Executable gates run: manifests, specs, docs, graph, security, dependencies, build.
steps.output: Pass/fail per gate; failures block the pipeline.
steps.artifacts:
gate reports in .arah/audit/
CI check results

steps.name: Signal
steps.description: Agents and cells communicate through persistent, typed, append-only events — consultation, verdicts, handoffs.
steps.output: Durable coordination visible to every tool.

steps.name: Audit
steps.description: Every action, result, autonomy decision and session event is recorded in the ledger.

steps.name: Evolve
steps.description: The harness analyzes ledger, signals and telemetry to propose improvements — new domains, skill changes, policy adjustments.

steps.name: Human Review
steps.description: Humans review proposals and changes in pull requests. Merge authority is human, always.

steps.name: Regenerate
steps.description: Homeostasis: update the kernel, re-run discovery, sync domains, regenerate graphs, validate the installation.
steps.artifacts:
updated kernel files
docs/_meta/graph.json
doctor report

demo.title: The path is identified
demo.body: A diff arrives touching backend/payments/**. Choreography matches the path against domain contracts and resolves which cells this change belongs to.
demo.terminal:
$ git diff --name-only
backend/payments/refunds.ts
backend/payments/refunds.spec.ts

choreography match:
  domain: payments
  rule:   "backend/payments/**"

demo.title: Related agents are selected
demo.body: The domain contract names its operational agent and consultative specialist. No other agent is activated — scope is explicit.
demo.terminal:
activate:
  agents:   [backend-payments]
  advisors: [payments-specialist]
excluded: 12 other agents (out of scope)

demo.title: Authorized skills are loaded
demo.body: The agent may only use the skills its manifest declares. Capabilities are versioned procedures, not improvisation.
demo.terminal:
skills loaded for backend-payments:
  ✓ test-first
  ✓ refactor
  ✗ schema-migration (not authorized)

demo.title: Domain specialists are consulted
demo.body: Before changing refund logic, the operational agent consults the payments specialist through the signal bus.
demo.terminal:
→ signal consultation.request
   to: payments-specialist
← signal consultation.answer
   "refunds must preserve idempotency;
    spec required before code"

demo.title: Gates execute
demo.body: Manifests, specs, docs, graph, security, dependencies and build are validated as code. A failing gate stops the flow here.
demo.terminal:
$ arah sync-check
✓ manifests   ✓ specs
✓ docs        ✓ graph
✓ security    ✓ dependencies
✓ build
gates: 7/7 passed

demo.title: Events are recorded
demo.body: Every coordination step is appended to the bus — typed, timestamped, portable across tools and sessions.

demo.title: Evidence is generated
demo.body: The audit ledger links the change to its session, autonomy level, gate results and consultation trail.
demo.terminal:
.arah/audit/run-8f31/
  session.json    autonomy: propose-only
  gates.report    7/7
  consultation.md payments-specialist
  diff.summary

demo.title: The change is prepared for PR
demo.body: The branch, evidence and gate results are packaged into a pull request. The agent proposes; it cannot merge.
demo.terminal:
$ gh pr create
PR #147: refunds idempotency guard
  evidence: .arah/audit/run-8f31/
  gates: 7/7 · autonomy: propose-only

demo.title: A human reviews and decides
demo.body: A human reviews the proposal with its full evidence trail and holds the merge authority. The repository remembers everything.
demo.terminal:
review: @maintainer
decision: approve + merge
ledger: + selection.merge #147

Agents propose. Humans select.
The repository remembers.
